title: Salesforce Automation Tools
intro: Automation is the cornerstone of an efficient Salesforce org. It lets businesses streamline processes, enforce data integrity, and cut down manual entry errors. In modern Salesforce development, declarative tools are strongly preferred over programmatic ones for standard use cases.

sections.heading: 3.1 Quick Actions, Page Layouts & Record Types

points.text: Let users rapidly create or update records, log calls, send emails, or invoke Lightning components. Can be global or object-specific.
Control which fields, related lists, and buttons appear on a record page. UI-level only — this can be bypassed via the API.
Offer different business processes, picklist values, and page layouts to different users based on their profile.
Enforce data quality by conditionally blocking record saves. They evaluate a formula on create/update — if it returns True, an error shows and the transaction rolls back.

Route records through a sequence of steps for approval by designated users. They can lock records from editing during the cycle, and trigger actions (Email Alerts, Field Updates, Outbound Messages, Tasks) on approval or rejection.

sections.heading: 3.3 Declarative Programming with Flows
sections.intro: With Process Builder and Workflow Rules retired, Flow is the premier declarative automation tool.

points.text: Guide users through a multi-step UI to collect and update data.
Fire automatically on record create, update, or delete. Run before save (fast field updates) or after save (update related records / perform actions).
Run in the background at a set time and frequency for a batch of records.
Invoked by Apex, the REST API, or other Flows without any user interaction.

sections.heading: 3.4 Flow Components
sections.intro: Flows are built from a visual interface composed of:

points.text: Actions (create/update/delete records), Logic (assignments, decisions, loops), and Data (getting records).
Variables, constants, formulas, and choices used to store and manipulate data during execution.
The lines that define the execution path between elements.
Always query for the ID, or use custom labels/metadata instead.
Flows auto-bulkify DML and SOQL, but never place a "Get Records" or "Update Records" element inside a Loop.
Use Fault Paths to catch exceptions and notify admins.

sections.heading: 3.6 Invocable Methods in Flows
sections.intro: When Flow can't achieve a complex requirement, it can call Apex.

points.text: Use the @InvocableMethod annotation on an Apex method.
Only one invocable method is allowed per class.
The method must be static and public or global.
It accepts a List<T> of inputs and returns a List<T> of outputs — keeping it bulkification-safe for Flow.

title: Apex Programming
intro: Apex is a strongly typed, object-oriented language that executes on Salesforce's servers. It has Java-like syntax and acts as the controller in Salesforce's MVC architecture.

points.text: Case-insensitive.
Statements end with a semicolon (;).
Strongly typed — variable data types must be declared.
Multi-tenant aware, with strictly enforced governor limits.
Lets developers run ad-hoc Apex without writing a formal class — handy for debugging, data fixes, or testing logic. Code here runs in the user's context and still consumes governor limits.
Integer, Double, Long, Decimal, Boolean, String, Date, Datetime, Time, Id, Blob — all are objects and default to null.
Represent Salesforce records, e.g. Account a = new Account();
Abstract data types storing a finite set of identifiers.

sections.heading: 4.4 Collections
sections.intro: Apex provides three primary collection types:

points.text: Ordered, allow duplicates, accessed via a 0-based index.
Unordered, unique elements — highly optimized for .contains() checks.
Key-value pairs with unique keys — great for pairing record IDs with the actual sObject.
Standard conditional execution.
Evaluates a single expression against multiple values — Apex's version of switch-case.
for (Integer i = 0; i < 10; i++) · for (String name : namesList) · for (Account acc : [SELECT Id FROM Account]) — the SOQL for-loop, optimized for heap space.

title: Data Manipulation Language (DML)
intro: DML is the toolset used to modify data in Salesforce — inserting, updating, and removing records safely and at scale.

points.text: Core statements: insert, update, upsert, delete, undelete, merge.
150 statements per synchronous transaction; 10,000 records processed per transaction.
insert accountList; runs "all or nothing" — if one record fails, the entire transaction rolls back.
Setting allOrNone to false allows partial success: successful records commit, and failures are returned in an error array.

sections.heading: 5.3 Schema Classes & Methods
sections.intro: Used to retrieve metadata information dynamically at runtime.

points.text: Schema.getGlobalDescribe()
Schema.DescribeSObjectResult
Useful for checking field-level security (isAccessible(), isUpdateable()) before performing DML.

sections.heading: 5.4 Database.Batchable Interface
sections.intro: For processing millions of records without hitting standard synchronous limits.

points.text: Collects the records or objects to be processed (returns a Database.QueryLocator or Iterable).
Performs the actual processing on the current chunk of data.
Runs post-processing operations, like sending a completion email.
Requires Database.Stateful to maintain state across execution chunks.
Thrown when dereferencing a null object.
Catches database constraint violations.
Thrown when assigning a list of records to a single sObject variable but the query returns 0 or more than 1 record.

title: SOQL and SOSL
intro: Salesforce gives you two query languages: SOQL for structured, relational queries, and SOSL for fast free-text search across objects.

points.text: Basic syntax:
points.code: SELECT Field1, Field2 FROM ObjectName WHERE Condition

points.text: SELECT Id, Account.Name FROM Contact
SELECT Id, (SELECT Id FROM Contacts) FROM Account
100 SOQL queries per synchronous transaction; 50,000 records retrieved.

sections.heading: 6.2 Salesforce Object Search Language (SOSL)
sections.intro: Used to perform text searches across multiple, unrelated objects simultaneously.

points.text: Basic syntax:
points.code:
FIND 'SearchQuery' IN ALL FIELDS
RETURNING Account(Name), Contact(FirstName)

points.text: Faster for free-text search, since it relies on search indexes rather than table scans.
Don't put SOQL or DML inside a FOR loop — bulkify by collecting criteria into Sets/Lists, querying once, and iterating over the results.
SELECT Id FROM Account WHERE Name = :targetName
Keep queries selective, using indexed fields in WHERE clauses to avoid table scans on Large Data Volumes (LDV).
